message:
perf(core): inline trait impls for IDs, sync guards, and OnceCell conversions

Add #[inline] to trait implementations on hot-path types that the
compiler cannot auto-inline across module/crate boundaries:

types/id.rs (8 sites):
- RegionId, TaskId, ObligationId: Debug::fmt and Display::fmt impls
  (called during tracing/logging on every state transition)
- Time: Add<Duration> impl and Debug::fmt

sync/rwlock.rs (2 sites):
- ReaderCountGuard::drop and WriterActiveGuard::drop — these are the
  read/write unlock paths called on every guard scope exit. Inlining
  eliminates the indirect call through the Drop vtable.

sync/once_cell.rs (2 sites):
- Default::default (delegates to new()) and From<T>::from (delegates
  to with_value()) — trivial forwarding impls that benefit from
  zero-overhead inlining at call sites.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
